reader.h(3) Library Functions Manual reader.h(3)

NAME

reader.h - Read ANSI ASCII frames from file descriptor, chunked by height.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


asciichat_error_t terminal_fd_reader_create (int fd, int frame_height, terminal_fd_reader_t **out)
Create a terminal FD reader from a file descriptor. asciichat_error_t terminal_fd_reader_next (terminal_fd_reader_t *reader, char **out_frame)
Read next frame from file descriptor. void terminal_fd_reader_destroy (terminal_fd_reader_t *reader)
Destroy terminal FD reader.

Detailed Description

Read ANSI ASCII frames from file descriptor, chunked by height.

Reads ASCII frames from any file descriptor (stdin, file, pipe) and chunks them by height (number of lines) as specified. Supports reading ANSI-colored ASCII art frames in batches.

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

February 2026

Definition in file reader.h.

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t terminal_fd_reader_create (int fd, int frame_height, terminal_fd_reader_t ** out)

Create a terminal FD reader from a file descriptor.

Parameters

fd File descriptor to read from (e.g., STDIN_FILENO, open file)
frame_height Number of lines per frame (required - used for frame boundaries)
out Pointer to store reader context

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK on success

Width is auto-detected from the first frame's line length.

52                                                                                                   {
53   (void)fd;
54   (void)frame_height;
55   if (out) *out = NULL;
56   return ASCIICHAT_OK;  // Stub - no stdin available in WASM
57 }

Referenced by session_client_like_run().






asciichat_error_t terminal_fd_reader_next
  (terminal_fd_reader_t * reader, char ** out_frame)


Read next frame from file descriptor. Reads frame_height lines
    from the FD and returns them as a single frame string. Returns NULL in
    out_frame when EOF is reached.




Parameters


reader Reader context


out_frame Pointer to store frame data (NULL when EOF, must free with
  SAFE_FREE)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success (including EOF)


Definition at line 64 of file
    platform/wasm/stubs/terminal.c.


64                                                                                           {
65   (void)reader;
66   if (out_frame) *out_frame = NULL;  // Signal EOF immediately
67   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
68 }
